pattern: Sorting lets group sizes grow as 1,2,3,... so both the size and the sum constraints are automatically satisfied, making the answer the largest g with g*(g+1)/2 students.
hints: If the grades are sorted ascending, assigning sizes 1,2,3,... in order keeps every group's sum strictly larger than the previous.
The only limit is the total student count: g groups need 1+2+...+g students.
Find the largest g such that g*(g+1)/2 is at most the number of grades.

edge_inputs.description: two students can only form one group
exactly a triangular count of three groups
one extra student beyond a triangular number still caps at the same g

stress.description: large random grade arrays of varying length
stress.note: answer depends only on length so values are arbitrary
